title: landolphia flavidiflora
intro: Landolphia flavidiflora, also known as the yellow-flowered poison apple, is a highly toxic and endangered plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant's sap contains a toxic compound called solanum alkaloid, which has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Landolphia flavidiflora include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, although its toxicity requires careful handling and preparation. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant's sap is often used to prepare herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, but its use should be strictly under the guidance of an experienced practitioner.
